core_mechanism: duality-based sharp asymptotic evaluation of the extremal value of a random high-dimensional bilinear/quadratic form, obtained by sandwiching it between a dual upper bound and a matching multi-replica lower bound that coincide as the dimension grows
brief_reason: The abstract determines the precise limiting spectral-norm error by pairing closed-form RDT upper bounds with a novel bilinear-quadratic lower-bounding mechanism and a two-replica bounding strategy that provably match in the large-dimensional limit.
